content: In India Who among the following has the power to pardon, reprieve or commute the punishment of any criminal?
explanation:
The President of India has the power to pardon, reprieve, or commute the punishment of any criminal. This authority is provided under Article 72 of the Indian Constitution.

Article 72: Power of President to grant pardons, etc., and to suspend, remit or commute sentences in certain cases

Article 72 of the Indian Constitution grants the President the following powers:

To grant pardons,

To suspend, remit, or commute sentences of any person convicted of any offense.

These powers extend to:

Sentences given in cases where the punishment or sentence is by a court-martial;

Sentences given in cases relating to an offense against any law relating to a matter to which the executive power of the Union extends;

In all cases where the sentence is a sentence of death.

Thus, the correct option is:

Option C: President

content: Which amendment of Indian Constitution removed the right to property from the list of fundamental rights?
explanation:
The 44th Amendment of the Indian Constitution removed the right to property from the list of fundamental rights. This amendment was enacted in 1978.

Before this amendment, the right to property was protected under Article 31. The 44th Amendment transferred the right to property from the category of fundamental rights to a new article, Article 300A, thereby making it a legal right instead. This ensures that while property rights are still protected, they do not carry the same weight as fundamental rights, which require more stringent protections against state interference.

This change was aimed to make land acquisition for public purposes easier and more efficient, reflecting the government's perspective that fundamental rights should align with broader socioeconomic goals.

Key Points:

Removed Right to Property as a Fundamental Right: 44th Amendment

Enacted in: 1978

Article before amendment: Article 31 (Fundamental Rights)

Article after amendment: Article 300A (Legal Right)

content: In January 2022, the Supreme Court conferred daughters with equal right to the father’s property even prior to codification of Hindu Personal Laws and enactment of the Hindu Succession Act in ______.
explanation:
In January 2022, the Supreme Court conferred daughters with equal rights to the father’s property even prior to the codification of Hindu Personal Laws and the enactment of the Hindu Succession Act in 1956.

The correct option is:

Option 4: 1956

content: Fundamental duties are ________ and not enforceable by law but are taken into account by the courts while adjudicating any matter.
explanation:
The correct answer is statutory.

Fundamental duties come under Part IVA and Article 51A.

These duties are statutory and are not enforceable by law but are taken into account by the courts while adjudicating any matter.

Fundamental Duties in the Constitution are inspired by USSR.

Japan is the only democratic country, other than India, to have Fundamental duties in its Constitution.

These duties were incorporated through the 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1976 in the Constitution on the recommendations of the Swaran Singh Committee.

Only 8 Fundamental duties were based on the Swaran Singh Committee recommendation while the 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act added 10 Fundamental duties in the Constitution.

Later, the 86th Amendment Act, 2002 was passed that introduced one more fundamental duty in the Constitution. Now there are Eleven Fundamental duties in the Constitution.

Rights and duties are correlative and inseparable.

content: Till which year did the High Court of Delhi continue to exercise jurisdiction over Himachal Pradesh?
explanation:
The High Court of Delhi continued to exercise jurisdiction over Himachal Pradesh till 1971.

Here's why:

Himachal Pradesh became a Union Territory on November 1, 1956. At this point, it didn't have its own High Court.

The Delhi High Court was given jurisdiction over the newly formed Union Territory.

It wasn't until January 25th, 1971, when Himachal Pradesh attained statehood, that it got its own High Court. This event marked the end of the Delhi High Court's jurisdiction over Himachal Pradesh.

content: Who among the following served the shortest tenure as the Prime Minister of India?
Which of the following is mentioned in the Preamble of the Constitution of India?

options.content: Liberty of thought, expression, belief, faith and worship

content: In January 2023, the legislative bill providing ________ reservation to women in state government jobs has been approved by Uttarakhand Governor, Lt Gen (Retd.) Gurmit Singh.
Under which Constitutional Amendment, the State shall provide free and compulsory education to all children from the age of six to fourteen years?
Which Article of the Indian Constitution empowers the Parliament to establish additional courts for better administration of laws made by it?
explanation:
Article 247 of the Indian Constitution empowers the Parliament to establish additional courts for the better administration of laws made by it.

This Article provides that notwithstanding anything in Chapter V of Part VI or in any other law for the time being in force, Parliament may by law provide for the establishment of any additional courts for the better administration of laws made by Parliament or of any existing laws with respect to a matter enumerated in the Union List.

Hence, the correct option is:

Option 4: Article 247

content: Which State Assembly passed a resolution to create a Legislative Council in 2021?
What is the duty of the Chief Minister, as mentioned under Article 167 of the Indian Constitution?

options.content: Communication of all decisions of the Council of Ministers to the Governor.
Appointment and removal of Council of the Ministers.
Appointment of the Speaker of the Legislative Assembly.
Appointment of Advocate General of the state in consultation with Attorney general.

content: Which Article of the Indian Constitution prohibits religious instructions in any statefunded educational institution?
Which Article of the Indian Constitution deals with the Right against Exploitation?
By which of the following acts was a bicameral legislature introduced in India at the centre level?
Part 3 of the Indian Constitution enlists how many groups of Fundamental Rights?
Which of the following can make provisions for Ancillary powers of the Supreme Court?
explanation:
Article 140 of the Constitution of India specifically deals with the Ancillary powers of Supreme Court.

It states:

"Parliament may by law make provision for conferring upon the Supreme Court such supplemental powers not inconsistent with any of the provisions of this Constitution as may appear to Parliament to be necessary or desirable for the purpose of enabling the Court more effectively to exercise the jurisdiction conferred upon it by or under this Constitution."

Therefore, it is the Parliament of India that can make provisions for ancillary powers of the Supreme Court by law.

The final answer is $\boxed{\text{Parliament of India}}$
